evals.prompt: Implement the remaining Node.js runtime tools from openapi.json: add sandbox_nodejs_execute and sandbox_nodejs_info in apps/agents/src/nexus/tools, wire exports in tools/index.ts, add them to codeTools, and update affected tests.
evals.expected_output: New nodejs tool files are created with OpenAPI-aligned zod schemas, HTTP calls use shared sandbox helper, barrel exports are updated, and tests reflect the new tools.
expectations: Creates prompt.ts and tool.ts for each requested Node.js endpoint
Uses OpenAPI constraints in zod schema
Returns normalized JSON from tool implementation
Updates tools-index and code-agent related tests

evals.prompt: Add browser tools following the established pattern: sandbox_browser_action and sandbox_browser_screenshot. Keep them in codeTools for now, and include practical output normalization for action results and screenshot metadata.
evals.expected_output: Browser tools are added with clear schema boundaries, endpoint wrappers call sandbox APIs, outputs are normalized for model consumption, and wiring/tests are updated.
expectations: Schemas map to endpoint request contracts
Response handling includes success and error paths

evals.prompt: Implement MCP wrappers from openapi.json: sandbox_mcp_list_servers, sandbox_mcp_list_tools, and sandbox_mcp_execute_tool. Ensure path params and body args are handled safely and tests cover tool export/wiring.
evals.expected_output: MCP tools are implemented with safe input handling, normalized outputs, and proper integration into the existing tool export and code agent pipeline.
expectations: Path parameters are represented explicitly in zod schema
Request body for MCP execute supports argument object
Test expectations updated for new tool counts and names
